preamble: The velar nasal [ŋ] can weaken and devoice to a voiceless velar fricative [x]. This transformation involves both lenition of the nasal closure and the loss of voicing, typically occurring in coda positions or before voiceless obstruents.

examples.note: Historical weakening of velar nasal-stop clusters into velar fricatives.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
